notable_pick_trades: Acquired 3rd-round pick from DAL in George Pickens trade. Multiple projected compensatory picks (3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th) for 2025 FA losses. Gave 2nd-round pick to SEA for DK Metcalf in 2025. Gave 4th-round pick to NE for Kyle Dugger in 2025.
roster_context: New era under HC Mike McCarthy after Mike Tomlin resigned. Went 10-7 in 2025 and lost Wild Card round to Houston 30-6. Aaron Rodgers (age 42, UFA) may or may not return — QB future is the biggest question mark. Released Jonnu Smith and Calvin Anderson as cap cuts. Traded George Pickens to DAL in 2025. DK Metcalf is the WR1 but WR room is extremely thin behind him. Secondary needs a near-complete overhaul at CB2 and safety. ~$42.7M in cap space with 12 draft picks including 5 in top 100 — massive draft capital to reshape the roster.

needs.explanation: DK Metcalf is the only proven weapon at receiver. Behind him, the cupboard is bare — Roman Wilson (2024 3rd-rounder) played just 5 offensive snaps as a rookie due to injury, Calvin Austin III is a UFA who may leave, and the Steelers have gone three consecutive years without a legitimate WR2. The team interviewed numerous WR prospects at the Combine and is expected to draft two receivers. This is the most critical draft need.
needs.current_starter: DK Metcalf (WR1), Roman Wilson / Calvin Austin III (WR2 - UFA)
needs.depth: Roman Wilson (unproven), Ben Skowronek, Malik Benson. Robert Woods (UFA) departed. Scotty Miller (UFA). Extremely thin.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (pick 21) — likely top priority for first pick

needs.explanation: Aaron Rodgers is 42 years old and a UFA — his return is uncertain (leaning ~60/40 to return per beat reporters). Even if Rodgers returns for one more year, the Steelers have NO long-term answer. Will Howard and Mason Rudolph are the only QBs under contract, and neither is viewed as a franchise solution. The 2026 QB class is considered weak, which complicates things, but the position must be addressed in some form — whether via mid-round draft pick, free agency (Malik Willis), or positioning for 2027.
needs.current_starter: Aaron Rodgers (UFA, may return) / Will Howard
needs.depth: Mason Rudolph (under contract). Rodgers' status unknown until free agency opens March 9.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 developmental QB if top value not available at 21. Could also address via FA or trade.

needs.explanation: Joey Porter Jr. is the CB1, but there is no reliable CB2. Darius Slay departed as a UFA after the 2025 season. Asante Samuel Jr. (UFA) is unlikely to return. Jalen Ramsey is under contract but is 31 and better suited as a nickel/safety hybrid — there are trade rumors involving him as well. Cory Trice Jr. and Donte Kent both spent 2025 on IR. Brandin Echols provides depth but is not a starter. The Steelers conducted heavy CB interviews at the Combine.
needs.current_starter: Joey Porter Jr. (CB1), Jalen Ramsey (nickel), vacant CB2
needs.depth: Brandin Echols, James Pierre (UFA — career year in 2025, could re-sign cheap), Cory Trice Jr. (injury prone), Donte Kent (IR in 2025)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2 — strong CB class makes this viable

needs.explanation: DeShon Elliott returns from a season-ending knee injury in 2025 as the projected box safety, but the free safety spot is wide open. Kyle Dugger (UFA, traded for mid-season in 2025) was inconsistent and is expected to leave. Juan Thornhill was released during the 2025 season. Jalen Ramsey played safety out of necessity but the team doesn't want him there long-term. The Steelers have just two safeties on the roster right now. Multiple analysts project safety as a top-3 need.
needs.current_starter: DeShon Elliott (SS, returning from injury), vacant FS
needs.depth: Only Elliott under contract as a safety. Chuck Clark (UFA). Jabrill Peppers (UFA).
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4, also likely to sign a FA safety

needs.explanation: LG Isaac Seumalo is a UFA and unlikely to return — he's in his mid-30s with recurring soft tissue injuries and beat writers predict the Steelers will move on. Spencer Anderson is the internal option but is unproven. Zach Frazier (C) and Mason McCormick (RG) are locked in as starters and played well. The need is specifically at left guard to replace Seumalo, plus general IOL depth with several OL free agents departing (Andrus Peat, Max Scharping, Jack Driscoll all UFAs).
needs.current_starter: Spencer Anderson (LG projection) / Zach Frazier (C) / Mason McCormick (RG)
needs.depth: Anderson is the frontrunner at LG, but OL depth evaporated — Peat, Scharping, Driscoll all UFAs
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3 — PFF recommends IOL in the third round

needs.explanation: Cam Heyward is under contract at $19.2M but turns 37 in May. He could be a cap casualty ($14.3M savings) or may play one final season. Keeanu Benton and Yahya Black haven't fully established themselves as long-term starters. Derrick Harmon (2025 1st-round pick) showed promise as a rookie. Dean Lowry, Isaiahh Loudermilk, and Daniel Ekuale are all UFAs, gutting the DL depth. Even if Heyward stays, succession planning is critical.
needs.current_starter: Cam Heyward (aging, possible cut/retirement), Keeanu Benton (NT), Derrick Harmon
needs.depth: Yahya Black (developmental), Esezi Otomewo (RFA). Lowry/Loudermilk/Ekuale all UFAs.

needs.explanation: Jaylen Warren is a UFA and the top back. Kenneth Gainwell, the 2025 team MVP with 1,023 all-purpose yards, is also a UFA and may find a better deal elsewhere. Kaleb Johnson (2025 draft pick) is the only RB under contract and showed some promise. The Steelers may need to add a complementary back, but this is not a premium need given Johnson's presence and the depth of the RB market.
needs.depth: Just Kaleb Johnson under contract if Warren and Gainwell leave

needs.explanation: Patrick Queen and Payton Wilson are both under contract and form a solid ILB duo. Wilson had an excellent second season. Cole Holcomb is a UFA. Carson Bruener provides depth as a 2025 draft pick. Malik Harrison may be cut. Could use one more depth piece but not a pressing need. Queen's long-term future is tentative but he's under contract for 2026.
needs.depth: Carson Bruener, Malik Harrison (possible cut). Holcomb (UFA).

free_agency_impact: Released TE Jonnu Smith (March 5, 2026) — saves $7M cap, leaves Freiermuth and Washington as only TEs
Released OT Calvin Anderson — minor cap savings
Aaron Rodgers (UFA) — decision pending, 60/40 lean to return per beat writers. If he doesn't return, QB need escalates dramatically
LG Isaac Seumalo (UFA) — expected to depart, creates IOL need
CB Darius Slay (UFA) — departed after 2025, creates massive CB2 void
CB Asante Samuel Jr. (UFA) — unlikely to return, further thins CB room
S Kyle Dugger (UFA) — inconsistent in 2025, expected to depart, FS position vacant
RB Kenneth Gainwell (UFA) — 2025 team MVP, may leave for better market elsewhere
RB Jaylen Warren (UFA) — starting RB heading to market
WR Calvin Austin III (UFA) — key depth piece, may not return under new coaching staff
DT depth gutted — Loudermilk, Lowry, Ekuale all UFAs
Mike Tomlin resigned; Mike McCarthy hired as HC — complete coaching overhaul changes scheme fit
Patrick Graham hired as DC (replacing Teryl Austin) — shifting from 3-4 base toward more multiple/hybrid fronts
George Pickens traded to DAL in May 2025 — received 2026 3rd-round pick, but left WR room thin behind Metcalf
Acquired DK Metcalf from SEA in March 2025 — 5-year, $150M extension, locked in as WR1
T.J. Watt trade rumors ongoing but unlikely — massive $42M/yr contract is hard to move
scheme_fit_notes: Under new DC Patrick Graham, Pittsburgh is expected to shift from Teryl Austin's base 3-4 to a more multiple, hybrid defensive scheme. Graham ran multiple fronts in Las Vegas and New York. This means EDGE prospects with versatility (can rush and drop) have elevated value. CB prospects who can play press or zone are important as Graham uses both. On offense, Mike McCarthy will call plays himself — his offenses historically favor vertical passing, play-action, and need a true WR2 opposite a WR1. IOL prospects who can pass protect are essential for McCarthy's dropback-heavy scheme. The weak 2026 QB class may push the Steelers toward a developmental mid-round QB pick (Round 3) rather than reaching in Round 1.
